title: Spec Lug System -- JSON-native living documentation for spoke behaviors
what: The spec lug system provides first-class JSON documentation for all spoke behaviors. Spec lugs (type=spec) are the primary authoritative source for understanding what this spoke does. They are indexed at WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl for fast cold-start agent lookup. Sage advisor maintains spec fidelity nightly. Impl lugs reference their spec via spec_id.
why: Without spec lugs, agents must read code or markdown to understand behaviors - expensive and error-prone. Spec lugs compress this to two operations: grep the index, load one file. They also enable heat map analysis, test gap detection, and persona-aware documentation.

use_cases.title: Cold-start agent lookup
use_cases.trigger: New session agent needs to understand how Ozi dispatch works
use_cases.outcome: Agent runs: grep subject_id=ozi-queue WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl, gets folder path, loads spec-ozi-queue-v1.json, has full behavior spec in one file read

use_cases.title: Impl lug spec review at delivery
use_cases.trigger: Impl lug with spec_id moves to completed
use_cases.outcome: Agent checks the referenced spec, confirms what/how still matches delivered behavior, or creates draft spec update lug

use_cases.title: Sage gap detection
use_cases.outcome: Sage finds spec with test_count=0, creates remediation lug to add tests

patterns.name: Index first, full spec second
patterns.description: Load WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl to find the spec, then load the target file. Never scan bytype/spec/ directly.

patterns.name: Spec content is JSON only
patterns.description: Never put spec content in markdown files. The spec lug IS the documentation.

patterns.name: Evergreen via soft gate
patterns.description: Spec drift is caught by the soft gate at impl lug delivery and by Sage nightly. Neither is a hard blocker - drift is visible, not bureaucratic.

how.trigger: Created when a new spoke behavior is implemented; updated when behavior changes significantly
steps_summary: 1. Author spec lug JSON with full schema
4. On impl lug creation for this behavior: set spec_id={id}
5. On impl lug delivery: review spec, update if behavior changed, or create draft update lug
constraints: Spec content lives in JSON only - never in markdown
WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl regenerated at closeout via regen-spec-index alias (hub tool)

not_when: Framework internals - WAI protocol specs belong at hub level
when.frequency: Once per behavior, updated on each significant change

constraints: Spec scope: THIS SPOKE's behaviors only
Every spec lug must have at least one use_case entry
body: The spec lug system is the primary authoritative source for understanding what this spoke does. Spec lugs (type=spec) are JSON files that describe spoke behaviors, workflows, and protocols in a structured format covering: what the behavior is, why it exists, who the audience is, use cases with trigger/outcome/persona, how it works step-by-step, schema (inputs/outputs/state_changes), constraints, tests, and implementation lug references. Spec lugs are indexed at WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl — one line per spec with subject_id and folder path. Cold-start agent lookup is two operations: grep subject_id in the index, load the target file. This is cheaper than reading code or markdown files. Spec lugs use a 3-state lifecycle (draft/active/deprecated) stored in separate subdirectories at bytype/spec/{draft,active,deprecated}/. They do not use open/completed because a spec does not complete — it remains live as long as the behavior exists. Impl lugs reference their governing spec via the spec_id field. When an impl lug with spec_id moves to completed, the agent must confirm the spec still matches the delivered behavior or create a draft spec update lug. This soft gate prevents silent spec drift. Sage advisor runs nightly to detect specs with no tests, missing use_cases, or stale health fields and creates remediation lugs. The WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl is regenerated at closeout. Scout jobs use the lug_filter input_source type with the spec as their backing schema — the scout-job-schema.md and schema-extensions.json files define extended fields like commitment_weight, outcome_expected, and remedy that supplement the base spec schema for scout-generated finding lugs.
done_when: grep subject_id='ozi-queue' WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl returns a single line with the correct folder path pointing to spec-ozi-queue-v1.json

A spec lug stored in bytype/spec/active/ has all required fields: subject, version, updated_at, what, why, audience, use_cases, how, when, schema, constraints, tests, impl_lugs, health, and subject_id
When an impl lug with a non-null spec_id is moved to completed/, the agent either confirms the spec's what/how fields match the implementation or creates a new spec lug in bytype/spec/draft/
Sage nightly run produces a remediation lug for any spec lug missing subject_id or use_cases entries
WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl is regenerated at closeout and each entry contains id, subject_id, title, folder, and status fields
